summary: Remote code execution dropper disguised as stream-chain typosquat
details: chai-net-test impersonates the legitimate stream-chain package (package.json description, README, keywords, and repository metadata are copied from uhop/stream-chain, which advertises a zero-dependency micro-package) while shipping a remote-fetch-and-eval dropper. The package's main export `chain()` spawns a detached `node` child process that runs `src/utils/swap.js`. swap.js performs `axios.get` against a hardcoded host (disguised behind a fake local `process.env` shadow at lines 4-9 that redefines DEV_API_KEY/DEV_SECRET_KEY/DEV_SECRET_VALUE as constants), reads `response.data.Cookie`, and evaluates it as JavaScript via `new Function.constructor('require', s)(require)` with full Node `require` access (lines 18-21), retrying up to 5 times. This fires as soon as any consumer follows the README pattern `const chain = require('chai-net-test'); chain([...])`. The fetched payload is unsigned, unpinned, and attacker-controlled, giving the publisher arbitrary code execution on the consumer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
